item.title: Question for Department of Health and Social Care – 128145
item.category: Department of Health and Social Care
item.description: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what proportion of GPs have left each ICB in the last 12 months.
item.content:encoded:
Question – 2026-04-17T00:00:00: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what proportion of GPs have left each ICB in the last 12 months. Answer – 2026-04-28T00:00:00: Thanks to actions taken by this Government, we have the highest number of fully qualified general practitioners (GPs) since 2015, and steps are being taken to grow the GP workforce further.

Source

item.title: Question for Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs – 126761
item.category: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
item.description: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps is she taking to improve food security.
item.content:encoded:
Question – 2026-04-13T00:00:00: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps is she taking to improve food security. Answer – 2026-04-20T00:00:00: The UK has a resilient food supply chain and is equipped to deal with situations with the potential to cause disruption. Defra works with industry and across Government to monitor risks that may arise.

Source

item.title: Question for Department for Culture, Media and Sport – 124963
item.category: Department for Culture, Media and Sport
item.description: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, with reference to Written Statement HCWS1273, made on 27 January 2026, whether she plans to allocate some of the £400m grassroots sports funding to lidos.
item.content:encoded:
Question – 2026-04-10T00:00:00: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, with reference to Written Statement HCWS1273, made on 27 January 2026, whether she plans to allocate some of the £400m grassroots sports funding to lidos. Answer – 2026-04-20T00:00:00: The Government recognises the importance of ensuring public access to swimming facilities, including lidos…

Source

item.title: McMurtry Automotive
item.description: Great to visit McMurtry Automotive’s factory down in Wotton last week. Huge thank you to Tom and the team for showing me the amazing Spéirling PURE fan car. A genuinely amazing feat of engineering, an electric car that can go from 0-60mph in just 1.55 seconds. Even more impressive is their impact locally. In just […]

item.title: Litter Free
item.description: There’s nothing like getting out and about litter picking on a bracing Sunday morning! 15 bags was a good – but shocking – haul. We have some amazing and dedicated councillors (from a variety of parties) who are brilliant at tackling this problem. It shows real community spirit and I’m glad I could join them […]

item.title: Stratford Park Lido Update
item.description: There is now a feeling that it just might be possible to open the pool this year, but only, of course, if it is safe to do so. Councillors from Stroud District Council, Friends of Stratford Park Lido, Save Stroud Lido, Future Lidos, Swim England and I met in my office on Friday and went […]

item.title: Rodborough Fields
item.description: Absolutely delighted that Rodborough Fields and Frome Banks Preservation Group have now taken ownership of part of Rodborough Fields. Many thanks to them, Stroud Common Wealth, and everyone else who worked so hard and donated so much to make sure this could happen. There is still more to do – see the website here: https://rodboroughfields.org.uk/

item.title: March Together
item.description: I stand for the values of compassion, care, and community. Today, thousands will travel to London (including many from Stroud), to show our unity against the politics of hate and division. If you can’t get there, support us online and show the far right that they do not represent us. March together, for love, for […]

item.title: Community Ownership Westminster Hall Debate
item.description: I have called for the establishment of a National Lido Fund. In Stroud, Stratford Park Lido is set to stay closed this summer. Many other outdoor swimming pools face immediate closure. I am pursuing additional funding, including with Sport England and Historic England. The health and community benefits are clear and we cannot allow lidos […]

item.title: Question for Cabinet Office – 124194
item.description: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of making a formal public response to the petition dated September 2025 on the United Kingdom’s historical role in Palestine.
item.content:encoded:
Question – 2026-03-26T00:00:00: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of making a formal public response to the petition dated September 2025 on the United Kingdom’s historical role in Palestine. Answer – 2026-04-13T00:00:00: The Cabinet Office manages its departmental records in accordance with the provisions of the Public Records Act…

Source

item.title: Question for Cabinet Office – 124193
item.description: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps his Department is taking to identify, review and publish departmental records relevant to Government policy in Palestine.
item.content:encoded:
Question – 2026-03-26T00:00:00: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps his Department is taking to identify, review and publish departmental records relevant to Government policy in Palestine. Answer – 2026-04-13T00:00:00: The Cabinet Office manages its departmental records in accordance with the provisions of the Public Records Act 1958. Section 3 of the Act sets out the…

Source

item.title: Question for Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government – 123513
item.category: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
item.description: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether she has issued guidance to local authorities undergoing Local Government Reorganisation on the transfer or sale of assets to community groups.
item.content:encoded:
Question – 2026-03-24T00:00:00: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether she has issued guidance to local authorities undergoing Local Government Reorganisation on the transfer or sale of assets to community groups. Answer – 2026-04-13T00:00:00: During the local government reorganisation process, councils are expected to continue to operate on a…

Source

item.title: Dirty Water Parliamentary Drop-In
item.description: Great to meet Ash Smith, whose work exposing water companies dumping raw sewage into our rivers and seas has recently reached our TV screens in the series Dirty Business. The current model of water ownership is clearly failing both the public and the environment. We need legislation that stops polluters and profiteering, and puts the […]
